questions.text: I believe that my willingness to defer is valuable to those around me.
I find that caffeine, sleep deprivation, or other stressors don't dramatically alter my emotional state.
I believe that living for something beyond myself is the only way to find real meaning.
I can sense when two people are pretending to get along but actually dislike each other.
I feel that I have a naturally wide window of tolerance for emotional stimulation.
I feel that a flexible, exploratory approach to problem-solving leads to more creative outcomes.
When I'm moved by something, the feeling washes over me like a wave.
I hold the view that punctuality shows respect for other people's time.
I feel that moral attention to suffering is the most fundamental practice through which I express my values.
I believe that analytical composure during a human crisis is what turns good intentions into effective action.
I tend to focus on the task at hand rather than the interpersonal dynamics around it.
I believe that maintaining objectivity during distressing events is the practice that most effectively prevents good intentions from leading to unintended harm.
I hold the view that true freedom means not being bound by predetermined plans.
I tend to be oblivious to the backstory between people I meet.
I hold the view that self-investment yields the highest returns of any investment.
I would rather slow down and ensure everyone is on board.
I believe that a leader who hesitates loses credibility.
I find that I set boundaries naturally around my time, energy, and attention.
